source.author: SSA Catalog
source.notes: High-impact shawarma-style marinade for vegetables (and mushrooms/tofu): warm spices, garlic, citrus, and herbs.

ingredients.label: Apple cider vinegar
notes: Sub: red wine vinegar or white vinegar.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
notes: About 1 tablespoon; optional but recommended for depth and color.
If avoiding tomato, omit and add +5 ml olive oil.

ingredients.label: Smoked paprika
notes: About 1 teaspoon; optional for smoky roast vibe.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: About 1 1/2 teaspoons. Sized for ~900–1200 g (2–2.5 lb) vegetables.
If you salt vegetables separately, reduce to 5–6 g.

ingredients.label: Ground sumac
notes: About 1 teaspoon; optional but adds bright, tangy shawarma note.

ingredients.label: Honey (or maple syrup for vegan)
notes: Optional: improves browning on roasted veggies (about 1 teaspoon).

ingredients.label: Water (as needed)
notes: Optional: helps disperse tomato paste/spices if the marinade is too thick.
Add 1 tsp (5 ml) at a time.

steps.title: Whisk the marinade
instructions: In a bowl, whisk olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, garlic, and tomato paste (if using) until smooth.
Whisk in all spices, black pepper, salt, optional dried herbs, optional sumac, optional chili flakes, and optional honey/maple.
If the marinade feels too thick to coat evenly, whisk in a little water (5–15 ml total) until pourable.

steps.title: What to marinate (suggestions)
instructions: Works especially well with: mushrooms, cauliflower florets, zucchini, eggplant, bell peppers, onions, chickpeas, and tofu/tempeh.
Cut vegetables into bite-size pieces for even cooking and flavor coverage.

steps.title: Marinate time
instructions: Toss vegetables thoroughly with marinade in a bowl or zip bag.
Rest 20–30 minutes at room temperature for quick flavor, or refrigerate 2–8 hours for deeper seasoning.
If using delicate vegetables (zucchini) or tofu, 30–120 minutes is usually enough.

steps.title: Cooking guidance (notes)
instructions: Roast: spread on a sheet pan (avoid overcrowding), roast hot (220–240°C / 425–465°F) until browned and tender, flipping once.
Grill: skewers or grill basket works well; cook over medium-high heat until charred.
Stovetop: cook in a hot skillet with a thin film of oil until browned, then finish until tender.
For maximum browning, wipe off excess wet marinade and let surfaces dry slightly before cooking.

steps.title: Food safety
instructions: If you marinate in the refrigerator, keep vegetables covered and chilled.
This marinade is vegan-friendly if you use maple syrup instead of honey.
